methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Stardust Solutions is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $75M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Lanzatech, LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector, including names such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($75M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the subsequent round yields an implied valuation of $150M. The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient narrow comparables: while 9 same-sector peers exist, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts at a sample size of three or more, making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for this particular subsector and the lack of same-stage peer data to validate growth expectations. Any revised valuation should be reconciled once comparable Series B or later-stage transactions in applied climate tech are available.
